mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 1.04% is substantially below the inflation rate implied by CPIAUCSL at 243.79 (year-over-year ~3.4%), creating negative real rates. This accommodative stance sustains equity valuations and reduces demand for safe-haven Treasury bonds, causing 10-year yields to rise over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into risk assets.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over the 20-day window despite negative real rates, or Rank IC of FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL spread against yield direction falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 146,404 thousand with unemployment at 4.4% indicates a tight labor market. If PAYEMS growth decelerates below 100k/month over the next 60 days, it signals weakening demand and recession risk, causing investors to flee to Treasuries and driving 10-year yields downward as growth expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth falls below 100k for two consecutive months
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise despite PAYEMS deceleration below 100k/month, or the correlation between PAYEMS growth and yield direction is not significantly negative (|r| < 0.25) over the 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 473,513 million and housing starts (HOUST) at 1,092 thousand reflect solid consumer demand. Sustained growth in these series over the next 20 trading days signals robust economic activity, increasing inflation expectations and causing the Federal Reserve to maintain or raise rates, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline despite month-over-month increases in both RSAFS and HOUST, or the joint signal (both series rising) fails to predict positive yield direction in > 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 105.16 is near trend. A decline in INDPRO below 104.5 over the next 5 trading days signals supply-side weakness or demand destruction, reducing inflation pressure and causing the Fed to ease policy expectations, driving 10-year Treasury yields downward.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise following INDPRO declines below 104.5, or Rank IC between INDPRO and yield direction is positive (> 0.05) over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 16,872.8 billion (chained 2017 dollars) represents the current growth trajectory. If forward-looking demand proxies (RSAFS, HOUST) contract simultaneously, it signals a demand shock that will depress future GDP growth, causing Treasury yields to fall as investors price in lower terminal rates and reduced inflation.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise despite synchronized declines in RSAFS and HOUST, or the interaction signal fails to predict negative yield direction in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate at 4.4% is below the estimated natural rate (~4.5-4.7%). If UNRATE falls further below 4.2% over the next 20 trading days, it signals overheating labor markets, forcing the Fed to maintain restrictive policy and raising expectations for higher-for-longer rates, pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward.
regime_hint: tight labor market with inflation concerns
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline despite UNRATE falling below 4.2%, or the correlation between UNRATE and yield direction is not significantly negative (|r| < 0.20) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and the unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly determine labor market slack. If PAYEMS growth accelerates above 200k/month while UNRATE remains stable or rises, it signals wage-driven inflation pressures that force the Fed to keep rates elevated, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth exceeds 200k and UNRATE does not fall
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline despite strong PAYEMS growth (> 200k) and stable/rising UNRATE, or the interaction signal produces Rank IC below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 105.16 and inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 243.79 jointly determine supply-side inflation dynamics. If INDPRO accelerates above 105.5 while CPIAUCSL remains elevated, it signals supply-side strength that can absorb demand without additional inflation, allowing the Fed to cut rates and reducing 10-year Treasury yields over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above 105.5 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise despite INDPRO acceleration above 105.5, or the interaction signal fails to predict negative yield direction in > 55% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.
